rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in assume/guarantee expressions by ensuring boolean types are used for logical conditions.
Avoid placing assignment statements in component type contracts; use implementation scopes for such bindings.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; they must be in component type contracts.
generic_rules: Ensure all AGREE expressions evaluate to boolean types for assume/guarantee statements.
Use only visible AADL features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation-level contracts.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE clauses are properly resolved within the same annex scope.
Remove invalid or unresolved AGREE clauses rather than attempting to patch them with new architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE assume/guarantee expressions must be boolean-valued; avoid numeric types in logical conditions.
Assignment statements in AGREE are restricted to output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Component type contracts must not include implementation-specific guarantees or assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to correct type errors by changing expression types without addressing root causes.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; move them to component type contracts.
Do not add new architecture to fix unresolved identifiers; instead, remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: Component type vs implementation scope placement rules
